## What You Will Learn

**GPUs** can accelerate training by 10-100x compared to CPUs. Writing device-agnostic code ensures your model runs on GPU when available and falls back to CPU when not.

### Key Concepts
- **torch.cuda.is_available()**: Checks if a CUDA-capable GPU is present.
- **torch.device()**: Creates a device object ('cuda' or 'cpu'). Pass this device to tensors and models.
- **model.to(device)**: Moves all model parameters to the specified device.
- **Device Agnostic Code**: Writing code that works on both CPU and GPU without modification.

### Why This Matters
Training a ResNet-50 on CPU could take weeks. On GPU, it takes hours. Always write device-agnostic code for portability.
